name: eSUB Construction Project Management API
what_it_does: eSUB cloud construction project management REST API for subcontractors focused on field documentation, daily reports, and project communication. Enables AI agents to manage daily field reports and labor time tracking for construction project documentation, handle RFI (Request for Information) and submittal tracking for construction project communication, access change order management and cost impact tracking for subcontractor billing, retrieve material tracking and equipment utilization data for field resource management, manage project correspondence and meeting minutes for construction documentation, handle punch list and inspection workflow for quality control, access project schedule and progress tracking for subcontractor operations visibility, retrieve photo and document storage for construction field evidence management, manage labor productivity and crew management for workforce operations, and integrate eSUB with Procore, accounting systems, and construction ERP platforms.
use_cases: Documenting daily reports from field operations agents
Tracking RFIs from project communication agents
Managing change orders from subcontractor billing agents
Monitoring labor hours from workforce management agents
not_for: General contractors managing owner relationships (use Procore or Autodesk)
Construction accounting and job costing (use Foundation or Sage 300 CRE)
Large enterprise construction ERP (use CMiC or Trimble Viewpoint)
best_when: A specialty subcontractor (mechanical, electrical, plumbing, concrete, steel) wanting AI agents to automate daily field documentation, RFI and submittal tracking, change order management, and labor time capture to improve back-office efficiency and project communication.
avoid_when: DAILY REPORT ACCURACY AND LEGAL ADMISSIBILITY: Automated daily field reports with inaccurate labor counts, weather conditions, or work descriptions can be used as adverse evidence in construction claims and litigation; automated reports must accurately capture verified field conditions, not estimated or placeholder data. Change order scope ambiguity — automated change order generation from verbal or informal direction must include complete written scope description and price; automated change orders with vague scope create disputes over included and excluded work. Lien rights documentation — automated daily reports and correspondence may establish lien rights timelines; automated documentation gaps or inaccuracies in critical notice periods can waive subcontractor lien rights in some states.

auth.notes: eSUB uses OAuth 2.0. REST API with JSON. San Diego, California HQ. Founded 2009. Private (VC-backed, BuildGroup). Subcontractor-focused construction project management. 3,000+ subcontractor users. Mobile-first field documentation. Procore integration certified. Competes with Procore (for subcontractors), Fieldwire, and Raken for construction field documentation.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — per-user monthly subscription
pricing.paid_tiers: Monthly/annual subscription; team and enterprise tiers
pricing.notes: San Diego CA. BuildGroup VC. Founded 2009. 3,000+ subcontractor users. Mobile-first. Procore certified integration. Per-user subscription.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per OAuth client; documented in developer portal

known_agent_gotchas: DAILY REPORT LEGAL EVIDENCE ACCURACY: Automated daily reports must accurately capture verified field conditions (weather, labor count, work completed); automated daily reports with estimated or AI-generated conditions without human verification can be used adversely in construction disputes and claims

No webhooks — daily report submission, RFI response, and change order approval events require polling; no push notification for project communication events; implement scheduled polling aligned with end-of-shift report submission cadence

Change order approval threshold — automated change order submission must respect contract notification timing requirements (often 14-21 days from direction); automated submission after deadline notice periods may waive subcontractor additional compensation rights

Procore integration sync latency — eSUB bidirectional Procore integration has sync intervals; automated workflows expecting real-time data across both platforms must account for sync delay; implement sync status check before cross-platform data decisions

Photo and document storage limits — automated field photo upload workflows must track storage consumption per plan; automated mass photo upload without storage monitoring can hit plan limits and silently fail document capture

Labor hour accuracy for lien claims — automated labor time capture via eSUB may be used to support mechanics lien claims; automated timecard entries with rounding errors or missing crew member entries create lien amount discrepancy risk
agent_readiness.security_notes: Construction project management. SOC2. OAuth2. US. Field documentation and subcontractor project data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Construction field management. SOC2. OAuth2. US. Subcontractor project and documentation data.
